author: aarshshahoffchain
content: Okay, so we use HasBlobs to fetch the list of available cells and then use GetBlobsV3 to actually fetch  the cells ?

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: <@1000001410538151996> Were there any discussions with the other EL implementations on their timeline for this ?

author: danielknopik
content: ethrex seemed to be eager. Actually wast able to get to talk to everyone yesterday as everyone was heading to dinner already

author: danielknopik
content: I will implement this in lighthouse today and ask barnabas to deploy marius's branch so that we get an early feeling for the impact 💪

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: Wait <@1000001410538151996> Did you see the latest message by Kamil on the cell level deltas TG  group ? Looks like GetBlobV4 is much much faster than GetBlobsV3

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: If that is true, we can just get away with using GetBlobsV4 rather than using HasBlobs at all man.

author: danielknopik
content: sounds more like a bug in GetBlobV3. There is no reason why V4 should be 100x faster....

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: Yeah I agree, but if it is a bug in GetBlobsV3, might as well wait for the fix instead of going down the HasBlobs route no ?

author: marcopolo__
content:
The fact that several different EL implementations behave this way makes me doubt this is a bug. It seems like this is a expensive operation.

HasBlobs on the other hand does seem like it can be implemented efficiently using only in-memory data.

<@1425510242749251606>, to answer your question, you would use hasBlobs to get the availability bitmap and send that right away while you make a background request to getBlobs.

author: marcopolo__
content: <@477396362917576713> do you have the notes from yesterday?

author: bosulmun
content:
> HasBlobs on the other hand does seem like it can be implemented efficiently using only in-memory data.

It is true. The data on TG group is based on sparse blobpool branch, and getBlobsV3 under sparse blobpool is slower than that on master. Regarding the latency on getBlobsV3 in devnet, we can check otel on grafana to get more information. Still investigating how to enable that ...

author: .just_kk
content: In geth master benchmarks GetBlobsV3 is good. ~1ms or so. Not clear why it was slow in the  devnet

author: marcopolo__
content: how are you benchmarking this? Are you even touching the disk?

author: marcopolo__
content:

2. why is there so much variation in the blob count per slot? Is it due to the missed slots above?

content: 1. teku was shut down to reduced noise of non partial messages. As of today their implementation should be done, i should be able to roll it out soon
